lifecycle.remediation_appendage: Build the backend routes that E5/E9/E10 native-editor work is typed-blocked on: (1) FR native-editor ingestion - extend api/flight_recorder.rs beyond the closed RuntimeChatEventV0_1 shape (deny_unknown_fields, 3-variant enum, hardcoded actor_id runtime_chat, UUID-only session_id) to accept a versioned native-editor event family covering NativeEditorAction kinds plus the 5 un-emitted interop kinds (STAGE_EMBED_BACK, CALENDAR_EVENT_BOUND, ACTIVITY_SPAN_CORRELATED, LOCUS_REF_RESOLVED, LOCUS_REVERSE_LOOKUP); (2) GET /memory/pack returning the REAL ace::MemoryPack shape (memory_id/memory_class/source_refs - the native client model must be aligned in the paired frontend change); (3) POST /memory/proposals review-gated intake (proposals land as pending review items, NEVER direct memory commits). All PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed, no SQLite. Frontend pairing (emit variants + client model alignment + un-ignoring the gated E9 proofs) is wave-2 native work that this MT unblocks.

scope.summary: Backend counterpart for the native editors WP: FR native-editor event ingestion, FEMS memory-pack read route, review-gated memory-proposal intake. Discovered by the 2026-07-02 drift audit as verified typed blockers behind MT-036 (event emitter has no ingestible backend shape), MT-063 (no /memory/pack route; client shape drift vs ace::MemoryPack), MT-064 (no /memory/proposals route), MT-074 (5 expected FR event kinds emitted by nothing and ingestible by nothing). Survivor-forward ingestion (MT-093) intentionally stays WP-KERNEL-016 scope - do not fold it in here.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST flight-recorder ingestion accepts a versioned native-editor event envelope (documented kind vocabulary incl. the 5 interop kinds) with typed rejection of unknown kinds/fields; events land in the FR authority store and are readable back via the existing GET route; idempotency on event_id; proven against real managed PostgreSQL.

GET /memory/pack returns the real ace::MemoryPack item shape; proven with a seeded pack against managed PostgreSQL; response shape asserted field-by-field so the native client alignment has a pinned contract.

POST /memory/proposals stores a review-gated proposal (document_id + selection-range + content-hash provenance required, fail-closed on missing provenance); a negative test proves proposals can NOT mutate memory directly; proven against managed PostgreSQL.
No SQLite anywhere; all durable writes through the PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority path; cargo check + targeted backend tests green.

proof_targets: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml <new fr ingestion tests> (managed PG auto-boot)
cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml <memory pack + proposal route tests> (managed PG auto-boot)

gui_obligation.not_applicable_reason: Backend routes only; the operator-visible surfaces (FR pane, relevant-memory pane, proposal dialog) are owned by the paired frontend MTs (MT-036/063/064) which this MT unblocks.

user_manual_obligation.not_applicable_reason: No operator-facing behavior change until the frontend pairing lands; manual entries for the composed flows are owned by the frontend MTs per their contracts.

risks: Widening the FR ingestion surface can weaken the typed-event discipline (free-text smuggling through new fields).
Memory-proposal intake could become a direct memory write path if the review gate is bypassed server-side.
Shape drift between ace::MemoryPack and the pinned AC-109-2 contract re-introduces the exact client decode failure the audit found.
minimum_controls: deny_unknown_fields + closed kind vocabulary on the new envelope; free-text only in bounded, named fields.
Negative test: direct memory mutation via the proposals route must fail closed (AC-109-3).
Field-by-field response-shape assertion pinned in tests (AC-109-2) so frontend alignment has a stable contract.
